title: pelargonium × papyraceum
intro: Pelargonium × papyraceum, a species of succulent plant, is a hybrid of the Pelargonium genus and is commonly known as paper geranium due to its delicate, papery leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African and herbal medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pelargonium × papyraceum include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, calm coughs, and reduce anxiety. Pelargonium × papyraceum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
